name: selvedge
description: Change tracking for AI-era codebases — captures the why behind every change.
long_description:
Selvedge is an MCP server that AI coding agents call as they work to log structured change events. It captures the entity (DB column, table, function, endpoint, dependency, env var), the diff, and the reasoning — the intent that would otherwise evaporate when the agent session ends.

With human-written code, intent leaked into commit messages and PR descriptions. With AI-written code, intent lives in a prompt that disappears. Selvedge writes it down before it's gone, so weeks later you can ask 'when was users.stripe_customer_id added and why?' and get a real answer.

The SQLite DB lives locally in `.selvedge/` next to your code — your data stays yours.

tools.name: log_change
tools.description: Record a change to a codebase entity (DB column/table, function, file, API endpoint, dependency, env var) with the reasoning behind it. Captures the intent that would otherwise evaporate when the agent session ends.

tools.name: diff
tools.description: Get change history for an entity or entity prefix. 'users' returns history for the users table and all its columns; 'src/auth.py' returns all changes to that file.

tools.name: blame
tools.description: Get the most recent change to an entity — what changed, when, which agent made the change, and why. Like git blame, but for semantic entities and AI agents.

tools.name: history
tools.description: Filtered history across all entities. Supports since (24h, 7d, 5mo, 1y), entity_path, project, and changeset_id filters.

tools.name: changeset
tools.description: Get all events belonging to a named changeset. Use to reconstruct the full scope of a feature or task across multiple entities.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Full-text substring search across entity paths, diffs, reasoning, and agents.
